content.full_text:
82013.

August 20, 1979

Ms. Carol Syverson
Treasurer
California Young Democrats
6022 Wilshire Blvd.
Los Angeles, CA 90036

Dear Ms. Syverson:

Thank you for your letter of July 27, 1979 asking whether or not your organization has filing obligations under the Political Reform Act.

Section 82013 of the Act defines committees as "Any person or combination of persons who directly or indirectly receives contributions or makes expenditures or contributions for the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination or election of one or more candidates, or the passage or defeat of any measure, including any committee or subcommittee of a political party, whether national, state, local, if:

a) contributions received total $500 or more in a calendar year;

b) independent expenditures total $500 or more in a calendar year; or

c) contributions made to or at the behest of candidates and committees total $5,000 or more in a calendar year."

If the California Young Democrats has not raised or spent $500 or if it has not made in-kind contributions of $500 or more within a calendar year, it does not have filing obligations at this time.

Please do not hesitate to contact the Technical Assistance Division of the FPPC if you require additional assistance.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

State of California

Memorandum

To       : Michael Bennett, Executive Director                    Date:    August 14, 1979
           Fair Political Practices Commission

From  : Secretary of State - DAVID B. PITMAN, Manager
           Political Reform Division

Subject: Letter Regarding Filing Duty


The attached letter addressed to your office is returned.




Attachment

DBP:tf

California
Young Democrats

6022 Wilshire Blvd
Los Angeles, CA 90036

(213) 931-1161

July 27, 1979

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

To Whom It May Concern:

I talked with your office in Los Angeles yesterday about whether or not the California Young Democrats fell into the category of having to file a Title 9 report.

Since we do not make contributions to candidates, have not received donations or contributions in excess of $200.00 and only organize our membership for volunteer work they said it looked like we wouldn't need to file.

We will however, be showing up on other people's Title 9 reports for the small contributions made to us from them.

We want to fully comply with all regulations on this matter, so if there are any questions about the California Young Democrats or if a question arises about our filing a report, please let us know as soon as possible and we will be happy to provide the appropriate information.

Sincerely yours,

Carol S. Syverson
Treasurer, California Young Democrats

sections.question_synthetic: Must the California Young Democrats file a Title 9 report given that they do not make contributions to candidates, have not received donations exceeding $200, and only organize membership for volunteer work?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The document includes a transmittal memorandum from the Secretary of State's office forwarding the letter to the FPPC. No formal FPPC conclusion or advice is present in this document. OCR quality is poor in some sections, affecting confidence.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming letter from Carol S. Syverson, Treasurer of California Young Democrats, seeking clarification on whether their organization must file a Title 9 report under California political finance laws. The organization states it does not make candidate contributions, has not received donations over $200, and only organizes volunteer work.
